w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Icebreaker Plus — Premium Subscription & Memory Resurfacing
Based on Icebreaker v4.0 Final Specification
Icebreaker Plus subscription system + Memory Resurfacing (free)
Ready to implement — no open decisions

This PRD is written for an LLM coding agent. Every section contains the precise schema changes, API contracts, business logic, and acceptance criteria needed to implement the feature end-to-end. Do not infer — follow exactly. Where a decision was open, it has been made here and stated explicitly. Cross-reference the v4.0 spec for existing table definitions.
Two distinct features are defined in this document:
Icebreaker Plus — a paid subscription tier ($3.99/month solo, $2.99/month per person for groups of 4+) that unlocks six premium features gating real app value behind a low-friction paywall.

Memory Resurfacing — a free-to-all notification system that resurfaces past Motives at T+14 days and on anniversary dates. This is a retention mechanic for the entire product, not a monetisation lever. Every user receives it.
1.2 What is NOT being built in this PRD
Group invite link generation for group pricing (future PRD — handled in payment webhook)
The following tables and systems already exist per the v4.0 spec and must not be modified unless explicitly stated:
users — all columns as specified in E1.1
memory_cards — id, motive_id, generated_at, cover_photo_url, vibe_tags, prompt_question, prompt_answer, attendee_ids, format, created_at
memory_photos — id, motive_id, stop_id, uploaded_by, photo_url, created_at. Upload window: 24h from motive completion.
memory_resurfacing_queue — id, motive_id, user_id, scheduled_at, sent_at, status ENUM(pending/sent/cancelled)
motives — full schema as per E1.3
$2.99 / person / month (requires 4+ members on same group subscription)
4 people. Each person pays individually at the group rate. No shared bill.
RevenueCat (handles iOS App Store + Google Play billing, webhook delivery, entitlement management)
None. No free trial for Plus.

The group discount is activated via an invite link. The first person subscribes solo. They share a group invite link from within the subscription management screen. When 3 more people join via the link and subscribe, all 4 switch to the group rate and receive a prorated credit for the difference. RevenueCat handles entitlements. The Icebreaker backend tracks group membership in plus_group_members and updates RevenueCat offering via webhook.
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('active','cancelled','expired','paused')),
billing_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (billing_type IN ('solo','group')),
group_id UUID REFERENCES plus_groups(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ON user_subscriptions(user_id) WHERE status = 'active';
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
owner_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('forming','active','disbanded')),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
group_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plus_groups(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
ADD COLUMN is_plus BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
ADD COLUMN active_motives_count SMALLINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;
-- active_motives_count is maintained by trigger (see Section 2.5)
-- Photo limit is enforced at the API layer, not the DB layer.
-- Free users: max 4 photos per motive_id.
ADD COLUMN is_preset BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE;
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
label TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (char_length(label) BETWEEN 2 AND 30),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
-- Plus users only. Max 20 custom tags per user (enforced at API layer).
-- export_url: signed Supabase Storage URL, valid 7 days.
Add a requiresPlus middleware to the Hono API. It runs after validateJWT.
export async function requiresPlus(c: Context, next: Next) {
const userId = c.get('userId'); // set by validateJWT
return c.json({ error: 'plus_required', message: 'Icebreaker Plus required.' }, 403);
For features that are gated but degrade gracefully (e.g. photo upload), use a helper instead of middleware:
RevenueCat sends webhooks to POST /webhooks/revenuecat. This endpoint must be unauthenticated (no validateJWT) but must verify the RevenueCat-Signature header.
RevenueCat-Signature: <HMAC-SHA256 of raw body using REVENUECAT_WEBHOOK_SECRET>
INITIAL_PURCHASE → create user_subscriptions row, set users.is_plus = true, users.plus_since = now()
DO NOT set is_plus = false yet — user retains access until period end
BILLING_ISSUE → set user_subscriptions.status = 'paused', send email via Resend

Always verify RevenueCat-Signature before processing. Use crypto.timingSafeEqual to prevent timing attacks. Reject with 401 if signature does not match. Log all webhook events to a webhook_events table for debugging — include raw body, event type, user_id, and processing status.
users.active_motives_count is maintained by a Postgres trigger. It counts motives where creator_id = user_id AND status IN ('open','locked','unconfirmed').
IF TG_OP = 'INSERT' AND NEW.status IN ('open','locked','unconfirmed') THEN
UPDATE users SET active_motives_count = GREATEST(0, active_motives_count - 1)
ELSIF TG_OP = 'DELETE' AND OLD.status IN ('open','locked','unconfirmed') THEN
UPDATE users SET active_motives_count = GREATEST(0, active_motives_count - 1)
AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OF status OR DELETE ON motives
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_active_motives_count();
Free users: maximum 3 simultaneously active Motives (status IN ('open','locked','unconfirmed'))
Active count tracked in users.active_motives_count (maintained by trigger — Section 2.5)
Limit enforced at POST /motives (creation endpoint) — not at the DB layer
// Add to Motive creation handler, before INSERT:
const user = await db.select({ count: users.activeMotivedCount })
message: 'You have 3 active Motives. Complete or cancel one, or upgrade to Plus.',
When error is active_motives_limit: show bottom sheet with copy 'You've got 3 plans in motion — that's the max on free. Upgrade to Plus to keep going.'
Sheet has two CTAs: 'Upgrade to Plus' (primary) and 'Manage my Motives' (secondary, navigates to Motives tab filtered to active)
Do not show the limit counter anywhere proactively — only surface the wall when it is hit
Free users: max 4 photos per motive_id in memory_photos
Plus users: no limit (enforced only by Supabase Storage capacity, not by code)
The 4-photo limit applies per motive, not per user — if a free user uploads 4 photos, no other user can add more photos to that motive either
EXCEPTION: If any attendee of the motive is Plus, the limit is lifted for the entire motive. This encourages group upgrades.
// Check if any attendee is Plus
message: 'Free memories hold up to 4 photos. Get Plus to add more.',
On the photo upload screen, show current count: '2 of 4 photos' for free users
At 4/4: disable the add button, show inline nudge: 'Upgrade to Plus or ask a friend with Plus to add more'
If the motive has a Plus member, show no counter — unlimited is implied
Journey Motives (is_journey = true, journey_type IN ('free_roam','day_out','itinerary')) are fully defined in the v4.0 spec Section D and the motives / motive_stops schema
Gate: if user is not Plus, the 'Journey' option in the Motive type selector is shown but tapping it opens the Plus upgrade sheet
Once a Journey Motive is created, all attendees can participate regardless of their subscription status — the gate is on creation only
message: 'Journey Motives are a Plus feature.',
Journey option in type selector: show with a flame badge overlay indicating Plus
Tapping Journey when not Plus: bottom sheet — 'Journey Motives let you plan multi-stop adventures. A Plus feature.'
CTAs: 'Upgrade to Plus' (primary), 'Plan a single-stop Motive instead' (secondary)
Free users: memory card visible in-app only. No download, no external share link.
Plus users: can download the memory card as a PNG, or generate a shareable link (signed Supabase Storage URL, 7-day expiry)
The memory card PNG already exists in Supabase Storage (generated by Satori pipeline per v4.0 spec). Export does not re-generate — it creates a signed URL.
Validates: user is an attendee of memory_cards.motive_id
1. Fetch memory_cards row — verify attendee_ids includes userId
2. If export_url exists AND export_expires_at > now() + 1h: return cached URL
Memory card screen: 'Save & Share' button visible for all users
Tapping when not Plus: upgrade sheet — 'Save your memories forever. Export and share with Plus.'

Tapping when Plus: calls POST /memory-cards/:id/export. On success: show native share sheet with the signed URL. Also offer 'Save to Camera Roll' (downloads the PNG locally via expo-file-system + expo-media-library).
Plus users have users.is_plus = true. This field is returned in profile API responses.
The badge is cosmetic — a small flame icon on the user's avatar/profile card.
Visible to all users on: profile cards in Discovery, prompt match reveals, Motive attendee lists, and the user's own Profile tab.
Badge appears automatically when is_plus = true. No separate setting or toggle.
// Add to user profile response shape:
plus_since: string | null, // ISO 8601, shown as 'Plus since Month Year' on own profile
UserAvatar component: add optional plusBadge prop. When true, render a 16x16 flame icon (using app icon set, color #FF6B35) in the bottom-right of the avatar circle.
Pass plusBadge={user.is_plus} wherever UserAvatar is rendered
On own Profile tab: below username, show 'Plus member since [Month Year]' in muted text when is_plus is true
No badge animation. Static icon only.
Free users: select tags only from the vibe_tags table (is_preset = true)
Plus users: can create custom tags stored in user_custom_vibe_tags. Max 20 custom tags per user.
Custom tags appear in the user's tag selection UI alongside preset tags, labelled 'Custom'
Custom tags are user-private — they cannot be searched by other users and do not appear on other users' profiles
Custom tags count toward the 3-tag minimum for onboarding (step 2) and the 20-custom-tag max
Validation: label 2–30 chars, emoji optional single character
Logic: check count of existing custom tags < 20, then INSERT
Logic: DELETE from user_custom_vibe_tags WHERE id = :id AND user_id = userId
preset: VibTag[], // from user_vibe_tags joined to vibe_tags
custom: CustomTag[] // from user_custom_vibe_tags (empty for free users)
Tag selection screen: 'Create your own' button at top of tag grid, visible to all users
Tapping when not Plus: upgrade sheet — 'Express exactly who you are. Custom tags are a Plus feature.'
Tapping when Plus: text input sheet with label field + optional emoji picker. Character counter shown (30 max). On save: POST /users/me/vibe-tags/custom.
Custom tags shown in a 'Custom' section at the top of the tag grid with an edit/delete button
At 20 custom tags: hide 'Create your own' button, show '20/20 custom tags' label

Memory Resurfacing is a push notification system that brings past Motives back to life. It is free for all users and serves as a retention mechanic. It does not gate or restrict any feature. The existing memory_resurfacing_queue table (from v4.0 spec) is the foundation.

Memory resurfacing makes the whole app stickier. A user who gets a 'One year ago today' notification for a great night out is reminded why they love Icebreaker — and is immediately primed to create a new Motive. This is retention infrastructure, not a revenue feature. Gating it would reduce its impact for zero meaningful gain.
'Remember [Motive title]? 🔥 Two weeks ago — tap to relive it.'
'One year ago today: [Motive title]. Tap to see the memory.'
'Two years since [Motive title]. That was a good one.'
The existing table handles T+14. Extend it to support anniversaries:
ADD COLUMN resurfacing_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 't14'
-- Unique constraint: one pending/sent entry per (motive, user, type)
Job name: populate-resurfacing-queue. Schedule: daily at 06:00 UTC.
// Step 1: T+14 — find motives that completed ~14 days ago
// For each motive + each attendee: INSERT into memory_resurfacing_queue
// ON CONFLICT (motive_id, user_id, resurfacing_type) DO NOTHING
Job name: send-resurfacing-notifications. Schedule: every hour at :00.
// 2. Fetch user push token from users table
// 3. Check user quiet hours (10pm–8am local) — if in quiet hours, skip until next run
// 4. Send via Expo push notification API
// On push failure: UPDATE status = 'cancelled', log error
// Opens the memory card screen for that motive
Users can disable memory resurfacing notifications in Settings > Notifications > 'Memory flashbacks'
When opted out: UPDATE user_notification_preferences SET resurfacing_enabled = false. Job checks this before sending.
ADD COLUMN resurfacing_notifications_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE;
If a motive is cancelled or deleted after queue entry is created: UPDATE status = 'cancelled' on all related queue rows (via trigger or Inngest cleanup job)
If user deletes their account: CASCADE deletes handle cleanup via FK ON DELETE CASCADE
If user has blocked all other attendees of a motive: still send — the memory belongs to the user regardless
The upgrade sheet can be triggered from six specific moments. Each has custom copy:
That's the max on free. Upgrade to keep going.
Free memories hold 4 photos. Plus holds them all.
Multi-stop plans are a Plus feature.
Download and share your memory card.
Create your own tags with Plus.
Implemented as a React Native Bottom Sheet (Gorhom) — same library used for Motive creation
Content: icon (flame, #FF6B35, 48px), headline (from table above), sub-copy, feature list (3 bullets relevant to trigger), pricing row ('$3.99/month · Cancel anytime'), two CTAs
Primary CTA: 'Upgrade to Plus' — calls RevenueCat.purchasePackage(). On success: close sheet, show success toast 'Welcome to Plus!', re-fetch user profile to update is_plus.
Secondary CTA: varies by trigger (e.g. 'Manage my Motives', 'Plan a single-stop Motive instead', 'Not now')
Group pricing footnote at bottom: 'Have a squad? Groups of 4+ get 25% off — $2.99 each.'
appUserID: userId, // use Icebreaker user UUID
if (!pkg) throw new Error('No monthly package found');

users.is_plus (set by RevenueCat webhook) is the server-side source of truth. The RevenueCat SDK is used only for purchase flow and initial entitlement check on app open. Never trust client-side entitlement alone for feature gating — all API endpoints verify server-side is_plus.
A free user creating a 4th Motive receives 403 with error 'active_motives_limit' and cta 'upgrade_plus'. The Motive is not created.
A Plus user can have more than 3 active Motives simultaneously. No limit is enforced.
users.active_motives_count increments on Motive creation (status open/locked/unconfirmed) and decrements on status change to completed/cancelled. It never goes below 0.
POST /webhooks/revenuecat with event INITIAL_PURCHASE sets users.is_plus = true and creates a user_subscriptions row within 5 seconds.
POST /webhooks/revenuecat with event EXPIRATION sets users.is_plus = false. The user immediately loses Plus features on next API call.
A RevenueCat webhook with invalid signature returns 401. The event is not processed.
A free user uploading a 5th photo to a Motive with no Plus attendees receives 403 with error 'photo_limit_reached'. Photo count remains 4.
A free user uploading a photo to a Motive that has at least one Plus attendee succeeds regardless of photo count.
A free user attempting to create a Journey Motive (is_journey: true) via the API receives 403 with error 'journey_plus_required'.
GET /memory-cards/:id/export returns 403 for a free user. POST /memory-cards/:id/export returns 403 for a free user.
POST /users/me/vibe-tags/custom returns 403 for a free user.
GET /users/:id returns is_plus: true for Plus users and is_plus: false for free users.

The populate-resurfacing-queue Inngest job, when run, inserts a pending row for each attendee of each completed motive scheduled 13–15 days ago (for t14), 364–366 days ago (for anniversary_1yr), and 729–731 days ago (for anniversary_2yr).
No duplicate rows are created. Running the job twice in 24 hours produces no additional rows (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).
The send-resurfacing-notifications job sends push notifications only to users where resurfacing_notifications_enabled = true.

Notifications are not sent during quiet hours (10pm–8am local time). If a notification's window falls after midnight local time relative to the original prompt date, it is cancelled (status = 'cancelled'), not deferred.
Tapping the notification deep-links to icebreaker://memory/:motive_id and opens the memory card screen.
Setting resurfacing_notifications_enabled = false in Settings stops all future resurfacing notifications. Pending queue rows are NOT cancelled — the job checks the preference at send time.
PlusUpgradeSheet renders correctly for all 6 trigger types with the correct headline and sub-copy.
Completing a purchase via RevenueCat updates the client UI within one user profile re-fetch (no app restart required).
The Plus badge (flame icon) appears on the UserAvatar component for all users where is_plus = true. It does not appear for free users.
Custom vibe tags created by a Plus user appear in their own tag selection UI. They do not appear in search results or on other users' profiles.
All events sent to PostHog. Use the existing PostHog client from the v4.0 spec.
Implement in this order to unblock testing at each step:
Database migrations — all schema changes in Section 2.2, the trigger in 2.5, and the resurfacing_type column in 4.3
RevenueCat webhook handler (Section 2.4) — this is the foundation for all entitlement state
Active Motives gate on POST /motives (Section 3.2) — highest-frequency conversion moment
Profile badge — update GET /users/:id response and UserAvatar component (Section 3.6)
PlusUpgradeSheet component with all 6 trigger types (Section 5.2)
RevenueCat client setup in mobile app (Section 5.3)
Memory resurfacing — populate job, then send job (Sections 4.4–4.5)

Steps 1–4 are the critical path. The webhook handler must be live and tested before any client-side purchase flow is built. Use RevenueCat's webhook test tool (dashboard > webhooks > send test event) to validate all event types before launch.

w:settings.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du

w:numbering.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:styles.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du

w:webSettings.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du

w:fonts.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du
